producer.trustBoundary: Formal tool output supports only the modeled or proved scope, assumptions, and bounded search recorded in the artifact. It is not whole-product proof.

source.entrypoint: pnpm run verify:tla and pnpm run verify:alloy in CI or local validation
source.formalSurface: formal-summary/v2 plus assumption, scope, and counterexample metadata
source.modelScope: Inventory reservation invariants and tenant-isolation example state transitions, not production authorization code.

rawSignals.value: TLC completed with 0 invariant violations for ReservationInvariant.
rawSignals.rawAssertionBoundary: The result is evidence for the modeled invariant only; assumptions must remain visible.

rawSignals.value: Alloy found no counterexample within the bounded tenant/account scope.
rawSignals.rawAssertionBoundary: No counterexample within a bound is not a universal proof for production behavior.

rawSignals.value: Storage is modeled as atomic and does not include retry or deployment behavior.
rawSignals.rawAssertionBoundary: Assumptions are part of the claim evidence and must not be hidden in raw logs.

commands.evidenceUse: Model-check evidence for the reservation invariant within the stated abstraction.
Bounded counterexample search evidence for the modeled tenant transition.

claimsMentioned.rawAssertion: The TLA+ model checked the reservation invariant without counterexamples.
claimsMentioned.controlPlaneBoundary: This claim is satisfied only for the model scope and assumptions recorded in the formal summary.

claimsMentioned.rawAssertion: The formal run does not cover the production authorization middleware.
claimsMentioned.controlPlaneBoundary: The model explicitly leaves this production claim partial until separate runtime or integration evidence exists.

expectedArtifacts.purpose: Record tool result, model/proof scope, assumptions, and counterexample state.
Summarize raw formal-tool signals as report-only producer evidence before assurance aggregation.
Attach formal evidence to scoped claims without promoting out-of-scope claims to proved.
Surface formal lane evidence and residual assumptions in reviewer-first summaries.
Connect formal model changes to the affected claim and evidence scope.

knownGaps.summary: Formal raw output remains a descriptive example until a schema defines raw producer input.
Reviewer surfaces should expose assumptions and out-of-scope claims alongside formal results.

unresolvedNotes: ACP-GAP-001: Formal raw output remains a descriptive example until a schema defines raw producer input.
ACP-GAP-003: Reviewer surfaces should expose assumptions and out-of-scope claims alongside formal results.

reportOnlyFindings.summary: ACP-GAP-001: Formal raw output remains a descriptive example until a schema defines raw producer input.
ACP-GAP-003: Reviewer surfaces should expose assumptions and out-of-scope claims alongside formal results.

controlPlaneJudgment.reason: Report-only normalization summarizes raw producer output and routing gaps; it does not decide pass, proved, or approved status.
